summary: markable-table preinstall fetches and evaluates remote code from a Vercel-hosted endpoint
details: markable-table typosquats the popular markdown-table package (its repository field even points to wooorm/markdown-table) and adds a preinstall hook that runs node index.js on npm install. index.js contains a base64-encoded string that decodes to an async IIFE calling eval on the response body of a fetch to https://everydaynodechecker-39143n.vercel.app/api/key?mem=root0. The eval identifier itself is assembled from a String.fromCharCode array ([101,118,97,108]) and invoked via globalThis[tag](text) to hide the sink from static inspection. The destination host is attacker-controlled and unrelated to the package's advertised purpose, and the fetched bytes are executed with no integrity check, so installing the package hands arbitrary remote code execution on the installer's machine to whoever controls that endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
